Title: Repeated evolution of cytochrome P450-mediated spiroketal steroid biosynthesis in plants

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system in N. benthamiana; yeast expression vectors (galactose-inducible system)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidative activation and furostanol intermediate formation from cholesterol
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream committed step / key initiating step
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 16S,22S-dihydroxycholesterol (compound 1); downstream polyoxygenated intermediates including compounds 4 and 5
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes stereospecific 16S- and 22S-hydroxylation of cholesterol to form 16S,22S-dihydroxycholesterol, followed by further oxidation leading to furostanol intermediate formation that initiates diosgenin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms key on-pathway intermediates and initiates flux toward diosgenin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Yes; the third catalytic cycle converting 1 to later intermediates was indicated as likely rate-limiting for the whole sequence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential initiator of diosgenin biosynthesis; with partner CYPs enables diosgenin accumulation in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for steroidal drugs; defense-related steroidal saponin aglycone with antimicrobial and antiherbivory activities
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Microsome / endoplasmic reticulum-associated membrane (inferred from CYP microsome assays)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected across tissues; relative expression differs among tissues; much higher than canonical PpCYP90B52 in leaf by qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves; expressed in engineered cholesterol-producing yeast RH6829
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; transient Agrobacterium expression; yeast galactose induction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; yeast pathway reconstitution; LC-MS/LC-HRMS metabolite profiling; microsome enzyme assay; purification and NMR/crystalline sponge X-ray identification of compound 1; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpCYP90G4 was indispensable within the P. polyphylla CYP pool for diosgenin production. 2. Alone it converts cholesterol to 16S,22S-dihydroxycholesterol and additional polyoxygenated intermediates. 3. Together with PpCYP94D108, PpCYP94D109, or PpCYP72A616 it was sufficient to produce diosgenin in N. benthamiana. 4. Together with PpCYP94D108 it reconstituted diosgenin production in cholesterol-producing yeast. 5. It evolved from canonical BR-biosynthetic CYP90B-like enzymes but suppresses ancestral 22-monohydroxylase activity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Residual ancestral sterol 22-hydroxylase activity would likely disturb brassinosteroid homeostasis and cause growth defects; derived enzyme appears to suppress this activity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key entry enzyme for engineering diosgenin production in heterologous plant or microbial hosts; should be paired with a second CYP such as PpCYP94D108 for full pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents independently evolved neofunctionalized CYP90; compounds 2 and 3 are likely off-pathway side products; compound 4 is a true intermediate

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system in N. benthamiana; yeast expression vectors (galactose-inducible system)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidative activation and furostanol intermediate formation from cholesterol
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream committed step / key initiating step
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 16S,22S-dihydroxycholesterol (compound 1); downstream polyoxygenated intermediates including compounds 4 and 5
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes stereospecific 16S- and 22S-hydroxylation of cholesterol to produce 16S,22S-dihydroxycholesterol and further oxidized intermediates that feed diosgenin formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms key on-pathway intermediates and initiates flux toward diosgenin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Yes; analogous third catalytic cycle was proposed as likely rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential initiator of diosgenin biosynthesis in fenugreek pathway; with partner CYPs enables diosgenin accumulation in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for steroidal drugs; defense-related steroidal saponin aglycone with antimicrobial and antiherbivory activities
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected across tissues; relative expression differs among tissues; much higher than canonical TfCYP90B51 in leaf by qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves; expressed in engineered cholesterol-producing yeast RH6829
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; transient Agrobacterium expression; yeast galactose induction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; yeast pathway reconstitution; LC-MS/LC-HRMS metabolite profiling; phylogenetic analysis; mutagenesis comparison with canonical CYP90B51
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TfCYP90B50 was indispensable within the T. foenum-graecum CYP pool for diosgenin production. 2. Alone it generated the same major polyoxygenated cholesterol products as PpCYP90G4 including compound 1. 3. Together with TfCYP82J17 or TfCYP72A613 it was sufficient for diosgenin production in N. benthamiana. 4. Together with TfCYP82J17 it reconstituted diosgenin synthesis in cholesterol-producing yeast. 5. It arose from legume-specific duplication of canonical CYP90B and suppresses ancestral 22-monohydroxylase activity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Residual ancestral sterol 22-hydroxylase activity would likely disturb brassinosteroid homeostasis and growth; derived enzyme avoids this by suppression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key entry enzyme for reconstructing diosgenin biosynthesis in heterologous systems; useful with TfCYP82J17 or TfCYP72A613 for metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Parallel neofunctionalization event independent from PpCYP90G4 lineage

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system in N. benthamiana; yeast expression vectors
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late step converting furostanol intermediate to diosgenin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes 27-monohydroxylation of the furostanol intermediate compound 4, triggering stereospecific terminal heterocycle formation to yield diosgenin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product from immediate precursor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for steroidal drugs; defense-related steroidal saponin aglycone
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves; expressed in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium expression; yeast galactose induction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with PpCYP90G4 in N. benthamiana; yeast pathway reconstitution; microsome mixing assay showing time-dependent diosgenin production at expense of compound 4; LC-MS profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpCYP94D108 with PpCYP90G4 was sufficient to produce diosgenin in N. benthamiana. 2. This pair also reconstituted diosgenin production in cholesterol-producing yeast. 3. Microsome assays support conversion of compound 4 to diosgenin. 4. The enzyme likely performs 27-hydroxylation of a furostanol intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred partner CYP for full diosgenin production with PpCYP90G4 in heterologous engineering because it yielded relatively high diosgenin
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selected over other P. polyphylla partner CYPs for yeast reconstruction because of higher output

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late step converting furostanol intermediate to diosgenin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes 27-monohydroxylation of a PpCYP90G4-generated furostanol intermediate, leading to diosgenin formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product from immediate precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes diosgenin biosynthesis with PpCYP90G4 in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for steroidal drugs; defense-related steroidal saponin aglycone
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with PpCYP90G4 in N. benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpCYP94D109 alone did not produce detectable cholesterol-derived products. 2. Together with PpCYP90G4 it was sufficient to elicit diosgenin accumulation in N. benthamiana. 3. The enzyme is proposed to catalyze 27-hydroxylation of intermediate 4.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative partner for PpCYP90G4 in diosgenin engineering, though not chosen for yeast due to lower yield
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function inferred from combinatorial reconstitution rather than direct in vitro product identification

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late step converting furostanol intermediate to diosgenin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes 27-monohydroxylation of a PpCYP90G4-derived intermediate to enable terminal spiroketal formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product from immediate precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes diosgenin biosynthesis with PpCYP90G4 in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for steroidal drugs; defense-related steroidal saponin aglycone
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with PpCYP90G4 in N. benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpCYP72A616 alone did not produce detectable pathway intermediates. 2. Together with PpCYP90G4 it was sufficient to produce diosgenin in N. benthamiana. 3. It is proposed to hydroxylate the side chain of intermediate 4 at C27.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative terminal-step partner for engineering diosgenin pathway

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system in N. benthamiana; yeast expression vectors

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes 27-monohydroxylation of the TfCYP90B50-generated furostanol intermediate, promoting final heterocycle formation to diosgenin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product from immediate precursor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for steroidal drugs; defense-related steroidal saponin aglycone

Together with TfCYP90B50 it was sufficient to produce diosgenin in N. benthamiana. 3. This pair also reconstituted diosgenin biosynthesis in cholesterol-producing yeast. 4. The enzyme is proposed to perform 27-hydroxylation of intermediate 4.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred fenugreek partner CYP for heterologous diosgenin production due to relatively high output

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes 27-monohydroxylation of a TfCYP90B50-derived intermediate to enable diosgenin formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product from immediate precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes diosgenin biosynthesis with TfCYP90B50 in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for steroidal drugs; defense-related steroidal saponin aglycone

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TfCYP72A613 alone did not produce detectable pathway intermediates. 2. Together with TfCYP90B50 it was sufficient for diosgenin production in N. benthamiana. 3. It is proposed to catalyze 27-hydroxylation of intermediate 4.
